title: An Unquiet Mind
subtitle: A Memoir of Moods and Madness
author: Kay Redfield Jamison
themes: The terrifying beauty of mania — creativity, euphoria, and destruction intertwined
The devastating depths of depression — the price of the manic high
The struggle to accept a lifelong diagnosis and treatment
The role of lithium in stabilizing a life that would otherwise be consumed by mood swings
The gift of mania — energy, charisma, ideas — lost when the medication works
Love and partnership as anchors in the storm of mental illness
excerpt: It was a long time before I realized that the price of my manic energy would be devastating depressions that would leave me unable to work or even to get out of bed. Mania is intoxicating, seductive, and — if you are like me — far more interesting than a placid existence. But mania has a dark side. The moods soar, and then they crash. And the crash is not just a 'down' — it is a black, suicidal despair that makes the highs seem like a cruel joke.
